methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Kinetic Automation is a Series B robotics and manufacturing automation company that has raised approximately $31M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C). The broader comparable universe comprises 9 private robotics firms and 1 public company (277810.KQ).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation framework given data availability. The median valuation across the 4-company peer set is $1.6B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.8B. This method anchors the analysis given the tight stage and sector alignment of the selected comparables.

The triangulated valuation range supported by this analysis is $1.4B (low) to $1.9B (high), centered on a median of $1.6B. This implies a post-money range of approximately 45x to 61x the capital raised. Key uncertainties include Kinetic Automation's specific unit economics, customer concentration, and IP defensibility relative to peers, none of which are reflected in peer-based pricing alone. The single-method reliance (peer median) also limits confidence; revenue or EBITDA multiples, venture capital method, or DCF analysis would strengthen triangulation but cannot be applied without additional operational and financial data.
